other_observers: Only me, as far as I know.; Yes.
description: Two (possibly four) birds were noted. Two were quite close to the water's edge. The alternate bird had a black head with a very large golden band extending behind broadly behind the eye, apparently wrapping around the nape. Its neck was dark rufous, contrasting with the all-black lower regions of the head (or "face").  Its companion was a mess, plumage-wise, as it was in the (fairly early?) stages of the prealternate molt of its body feathers. It was just a gray and black blob. That said, its bill was perfect for Horned: bulbous and gently rounded above and below, with that telltale pale tip.  The two birds were about the same size and structure. Speaking of structure, the birds had that Horned Grebe structure about them: stouter and more-powerful looking than Eared, and much-less buoyant-looking. I find that Eareds practically float on top of the water, looking very puffy and feathery; Horneds, in contrast, tend to ride deeper in the water. These birds looked liked Horneds in that regard.  Two other birds, much farther out, might have been other Horned Grebes. I'm not sure. The light far out on the lake was very poor. They were Podiceps grebes, but that's all I'm willint to commit to.Behaviors: Swimming (generally away from me), with 2 pronlonged dives. They kept quite close to each other.Call: none heardPlumage: 1 alternate; 1 scuzzy prealternate
